title: coleus aegyptiacus
intro: Coleus aegyptiacus, also known as Clerodendrum colebrookeanum, is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and purple or blue calyx. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Coleus aegyptiacus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
